name: Vali Odin
description: Vali, son of Odin and the giantess Rindr, is a significant, albeit often overlooked, figure in Norse mythology. Born specifically to avenge the death of Baldr, Vali's destiny was predetermined from his conception. He was born just one day after Baldr's death and, remarkably, reached adulthood within that single day. As an adult, his sole purpose was to slay Höðr, Baldr's blind brother who unwittingly killed him, thereby fulfilling the prophecy and exacting vengeance. Vali's act of vengeance is crucial in the events leading up to Ragnarök, the twilight of the gods. Following Ragnarök, Vali is prophesied to be among the few gods to survive, alongside Baldr, Höðr, and Víðarr. He will then help to rebuild the world, ushering in a new era of peace and prosperity. Vali is often associated with archery, vengeance, and the renewal of the world. His swift maturation and decisive action highlight the themes of fate, duty, and the cyclical nature of destruction and rebirth that are central to Norse cosmology. While not as widely worshipped as other deities like Thor or Odin, Vali represents the unwavering force of justice and the promise of hope even in the face of ultimate destruction. His existence showcases the Norse belief in the inevitable return of order after chaos, and the importance of avenging wrongdoing to restore balance to the cosmos. Vali's powers include immense strength, rapid growth, and exceptional archery skills. He is a symbol of swift and decisive justice.
subtitle: Avenger of Baldr and Rebuilder of Worlds
symbols: The Number One (representing his single day of growth)

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Vali
keyMyths.description: Vali was born to Odin and Rindr specifically to avenge Baldr's death. He reached adulthood within a single day, ready to fulfill his purpose.

keyMyths.title: The Slaying of Höðr
keyMyths.description: Immediately upon reaching adulthood, Vali slays Höðr, Baldr's brother, fulfilling the prophecy and avenging Baldr's death. This act is a crucial step towards Ragnarök.

keyMyths.title: Vali's Survival of Ragnarök
keyMyths.description: Vali is prophesied to survive Ragnarök, the twilight of the gods. He will then help rebuild the world alongside Baldr and other surviving gods.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, including information on Vali's birth and role.
sources.text: A key source for Norse mythology, including information on Vali's birth and role.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that provide insights into Vali's role in Norse cosmology.
